author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXXXIX

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1. …Our Lord Jesus Christ speaketh in the Prophets, sometimes in His own Name, sometimes in ours, because He maketh Himself one with us; as it is said, “they twain shall be one flesh.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore also the Lord saith in the Gospel, speaking of marriage, “therefore they are no more twain, but one flesh.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One flesh, because of our mortality He took flesh; not one divinity, for He is the Creator, we the creature.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatsoever then our Lord speaketh in the person of the Flesh He took upon Him, belongeth both to that Head which hath already ascended into heaven, and to those members which still toil in their earthly wandering.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us hear then our Lord Jesus Christ speaking in prophecy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Psalms were sung long before the Lord was born of Mary, yet not before He was Lord: for from everlasting He was the Creator of all things, but in time He was born of His creature.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us believe that Godhead, and, so far as we can, understand Him to be equal to the Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that Godhead equal to the Father was made partaker of our mortal nature, not of His own store, but of ours; that we too might be made partakers of His Divine Nature, not of our store, but of His.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lord, Thou hast tried me, and known me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the Lord Jesus Christ Himself say this; let Him too say, “Lord,” to the Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For His Father is not His Lord, save because He hath deigned to be born according to the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is Father of the God, Lord of the Man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wouldest thou know to whom He is Father?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the coequal Son.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostle saith, “Who, being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To this “Form” God is Father, the “Form” equal to Himself, the only-begotten Son, begotten of His Substance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But forasmuch as for our sakes, that we might be re-made, and made partakers of His Divine Nature, being renewed unto life eternal, He was made partaker of our mortal nature, what saith the Apostle of Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith, “yet He emptied Himself, and took upon Him the form of a servant, and was made in the likeness of men, and was found in fashion as a man.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was in the Form of God, equal to the Father; He took upon Him the form of a servant, so as therein to be less than the Father.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast known My down-sitting and Mine up-rising” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What here is “down-sitting,” what “up-rising”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who sitteth, humbleth himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord then “sat” in His Passion, “up-rose” in His Resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou,” he saith, hast known this; that is, Thou hast willed, Thou hast approved; according to Thy will was it done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if thou choosest to take the words of the Head in the person of the Body: man sitteth when he humbleth himself in penitence, he riseth up when his sins are forgiven, and he is lifted up to the hope of everlasting life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lift not up yourselves, unless ye have first been humbled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many wish to rise before they have sat down, they wish to appear righteous, before they have confessed that they are sinners.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast understood my thoughts from afar; Thou hast tracked out my path and my limit” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 3); “and all my ways Thou hast seen beforehand” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “from afar”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: While I am yet in my pilgrimage, before I reach that, my true country, Thou hast known my thoughts.…The younger son went into a far country.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After his toil and suffering and tribulation and want, he thought on his father, and desired to return, and said, “I will arise, and go to my father.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will arise,” said he, for before he had sat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here then thou mayest recognise him saying, “Thou hast known my down-sitting and up-rising.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I sat, in want; I arose, in longing for Thy Bread.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast understood my thoughts from afar.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For far indeed had I gone; but where is not He whom I had left?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore the Lord saith in the Gospel, that his father met him as he was coming.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly; for “he had understood his thoughts from afar.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My path,” he saith; what, but a bad path, the path he had walked to leave his father?…What is, “my path”? that by which I have gone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “my limit”? that whereunto I have reached.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast tracked out my path and my limit.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That limit of mine, far distant as it was, was not far from Thine eyes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Far had I gone, and yet Thou wast there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And all my ways Thou hast seen beforehand.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He said not, “hast seen,” but, “hast seen beforehand.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Before I went by them, before I walked in them, Thou didst see them beforehand; and Thou didst permit me in toil to go my own ways, that, if I desired not to toil, I might return into Thy ways.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For there is no deceit in my tongue.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meant he by this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, I confess to Thee, I have walked in my own way, I am become far from Thee, I have departed from Thee, with whom it was well with me, and to my good it was ill with me without Thee.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Behold Thou, Lord, hast known all my last doings, and the ancient ones” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast known my latest doings, when I fed swine; Thou hast known my ancient doings, when I asked of Thee my portion of goods.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ancient doings were the beginnings to me of latest ills: ancient sin, when we fell; latest punishment, when we came into this toilsome and dangerous mortality.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And would that this may be “latest” to us; it will be, if now we will to return.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is another “latest” for certain wicked ones, to whom it shall be said, “Go ye into everlasting fire.”…“Thou hast fashioned me, and hast laid Thine hand upon me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Fashioned me,” where?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this mortality; now, to the toils whereunto we all are born.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For none is born, but God has fashioned him in his mother’s womb; nor is there any creature, whereof God is not the Fashioner.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “Thou hast fashioned me” in this toil, “and laid Thine hand upon me,” Thine avenging hand, putting down the proud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thus healthfully hath He cast down the proud, that He may lift him up humble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy skill hath displayed itself wonderfully in me: it hath waxed mighty: I shall not be able to attain unto it” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Listen now and hear somewhat, which is obscure indeed, yet bringeth no small pleasure in the understanding thereon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Moses, the holy servant of God, with whom God spake by a cloud, for, speaking after human fashion, He must needs speak to His servant through some work of His hands which He assumed,…longed and desired to see the true appearance of God, and said to God, who was conversing with him, “If now I have found grace in Thy sight, show me Thyself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When this he desired vehemently, and would extort from God in that sort of friendly familiarity, if we may so speak, wherewith God deigned to treat him, that he might see His Glory and His Face, in such wise as we can speak of God’s Face, He said unto him, “Thou canst not see My Face; for no one hath seen My Face, and lived;” but I will place thee in a clift of the rock, and will pass by, and will set My hand upon thee; and when I have passed by, thou shalt see My back parts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And from these words there ariseth another enigma, that is, an obscure figure of the truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When I have passed by,” saith God, “thou shalt see My back parts;” as though He hath on one side His face, on another His back.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Far be it from us to have any such thoughts of that Majesty!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whoso hath such thoughts of God, what advantageth it him that the temples are closed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is building an idol in his own heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In these words then are mighty mysteries.…They who raged against the Lord, whom they saw, now seek counsel how they may be saved; and it is said to them, “Repent, and be baptized every one of you in the Name of Jesus Christ, and your sins shall be forgiven you.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, they saw the back parts of Him, whose face they could not see.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For His Hand was upon their eyes, not for ever, but while He passed by.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After He had passed He took away His Hand from their eyes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the hand was taken from their eyes, they say to the disciples, “What shall we do?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At first they are fierce, afterwards loving; at first angry, afterwards fearful; at first hard, then pleasant; at first blind, then enlightened.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold thou findest that the runaway in a far country cannot escape His eyes, from whom he fleeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whither can he go now, whose “limit is tracked out”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, what saith he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Whither shall I go from Thy Spirit?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who can in the world flee from that Spirit, with whom the world is filled?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And whither shall I flee from Thy Face?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He seeketh a place whither to flee from the wrath of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What place will shelter God’s runaway?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Men who shelter runaways, ask them from whom they have fled; and when they find any one a slave of some master less powerful than themselves, him they shelter as it were without any fear, saying in their hearts, “he hath not a master by whom he can be tracked out.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when they are told of a powerful master, they either shelter not, or they shelter with great fear, because even a powerful man can be deceived.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where is God not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who can deceive God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whom doth not God see?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From whom doth not God demand His runaway?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whither then shall that runaway go from the Face of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He turneth him hither and thither, as though seeking a spot to flee to.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If I go up,” saith he, “to heaven, Thou art there: if I go down to Hades, Thou art present” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At length, miserable runaway, thou hast learnt, that by no means canst thou make thyself far from Him, from whom thou hast wished to remove far away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, He is everywhere; thou, whither wilt thou go?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath found counsel, and that inspired by Him, who now deigneth to recall him.…If by sinning I go down to the depths of wickednesses, and spurn to confess, saying, “Who seeth me” (for “in Hades who shall confess to Thee?”) there also Thou art present, to punish.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whither then shall I go that I may flee from Thy presence, that is, not find Thee angry?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This plan he found: So will I flee, saith he, from Thy Face, so will I flee from Thy Spirit; from Thy avenging Spirit, Thy avenging Face thus will I flee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If I take again my wings right forward, and abide in the utmost parts of the sea” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So can I flee from Thy Face.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he will flee to the utmost part of the sea from the Face of God, will not He from whom he fleeth be there?…For what are “the utmost parts of the sea,” but the end of the world?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thither let us now flee in hope and longing, with the wings of twofold love; let us have no rest, save in “the utmost parts of the sea.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if elsewhere we wish for rest, we shall be hurled headlong into the sea.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us fly even to the ends of the sea, let us bear ourselves aloft on the wings of twofold love; meanwhile let us flee to God in hope, and in faithful hope let us meditate on that “end of the sea.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now listen who may bring us thither.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The very same One whose face in wrath we wish to flee from.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Even thither shall Thy hand conduct me, and Thy right hand lead me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This let us meditate on, beloved brethren, let this be our hope, this our consolation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us take again through love the wings we lost through lust.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For lust was the lime of our wings, it clashed us down from the freedom of our sky, that is, the free breezes of the Spirit of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence dashed down we lost our wings, and were, so to speak, imprisoned in the power of the fowler; thence “He” redeemed us with His Blood, whom we fled from to be caught.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He maketh us wings of His commandments; we raise them aloft now free from lime.…Needs then must we have wings, and needs must He conduct us, for He is our Helper.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have free-will; but even with that free-will what can we do, unless He help us who commandeth us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And considering the length of the way, what said he to himself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I said, Peradventure the darkness shall overwhelm me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, now I have believed in Christ, now am I wafted aloft on the wings of twofold love.…Regarding the length of the way, I said to myself, “And the night was light in my delight.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The night was made to me light, because in the night I despaired of being able to cross so great a sea, to surmount so long a journey, to reach the utmost parts by persevering to the end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thanks to Him who sought me when a runaway, who smote my back with strokes of the scourge, who by calling me recalled me from destruction, who made my night light.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is night so long as we are passing through this life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How was the night made light?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because Christ came down into the night.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For darkness shall not be darkened by Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not thou then darken thy darkness; God darkeneth it not, but enlighteneth it yet more; for to Him is said in another Psalm, “Thou, Lord, shalt light my candle: my God shall enlighten my darkness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But who are they who “darken their darkness,” which God darkeneth not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Evil men, perverse men; when they sin, verily they are darkness; when they confess not their sins which they have committed but go on to defend them, they “darken their darkness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore now if thou hast sinned thou art in darkness, but by confessing thy darkness thou shalt obtain to have thy darkness lightened; by defending thy darkness, thou shalt “darken thy darkness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And where wilt thou escape from double darkness, who wast in difficulty in single darkness?…Let us not “darken our darkness” by defending our sins, and “the night shall be light in our delight.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And night shall be lightened as the day.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Night, as the day.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Day” to us is worldly prosperity, night adversity in this world: but, if we learn that it is by the desert of our sins that we suffer adversities, and our Father’s scourges are sweet to us, that the Judge’s sentence may not be bitter to us, so shall we find the darkness of this night to be, as it were, the light of this night.…But when Christ our Lord has come, and has dwelt in the soul by faith, and promised other light, and inspired and given patience, and warned a man not to delight in prosperity or to be crushed by adversity, the man, being faithful, begins to treat this world with indifference; not to be lifted up when prosperity befalls him, nor crushed when adversity, but in all things to praise God, not only when he aboundeth, but also when he loseth; not only when he is in health, but also when he is sick.…“As is His darkness, so is also His light.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His darkness overwhelms me not, because His light lifts me not up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou, O Lord, hast possessed my reins” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Possessor is within; He occupieth not only the heart, but also the reins; not only the thoughts, but also the delights: He then possesseth that whence I should feel delight at any light in this world: He occupieth my reins: I know not delight, save from the inward light of His Wisdom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou not delight that thy affairs are very prosperous, times fortunate to thee? dost thou not delight in honour, in riches, in thy family?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I do not,” saith he.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “Thou hast possessed my reins, O Lord; Thou hast taken me up from my mother’s womb.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: While I was in my mother’s womb, I did not regard with indifference the darkness of that night and the light of that night.…Now, having been taken up from the womb of that our mother, we look on them with indifference, and say, “As is His darkness, so is also His light.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Neither doth earthly prosperity make us happy, nor earthly adversity wretched.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We must maintain righteousness, love faith, hope in God, love God, love our neighbours also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After these toils we shall have unfailing light, day without setting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fleeting is all the light and darkness of this night.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will confess to Thee, O Lord, for terribly hast Thou been made wonderful: wondrous are Thy works, and my soul knoweth it right well” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Aforetime “Thy knowledge was made wonderful from me, it had waxed great, nor could I attain unto it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From me then “it had waxed great.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence doth “my soul” now “know right well,” save because the “night is light in my delight?” save because Thy grace hath come unto me, and enlightened my darkness? save because Thou hast possessed my reins? save because Thou hast taken me up from my mother’s womb?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My bone is not hid from Thee, which Thou hast made in secret” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His bone,” he saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What the people call ossum, is in Latin called os.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the word in the Greek.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we might think the word os is here the one which makes in the plural ora, not os (short), which makes ossa.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith then, I have a certain bone (ossum) in secret.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this word let us prefer to use; better is it that scholars find fault with us, than that the people understand us not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There is then,” saith he, “a certain bone of mine, within, hidden; Thou hast made within a bone for me in secret, yet is it not hidden from Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In secret hast Thou made it, but hast Thou therefore hidden it from Thyself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This my bone made by Thee in secret men see not, men know not: Thou knowest, who hast made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What “bone” then meaneth he, brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us seek it, it is “in secret.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because as Christians we are speaking in the Name of the Lord to Christians, now we find what bone is of this kind.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a sort of inward strength; for strength and fortitude are understood to be in the bones.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is then a sort of inward strength of the soul, wherein it is not broken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatever tortures, whatever tribulations, whatever adversities rage around, that which God hath made strong in secret in us, cannot be broken, yieldeth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For by God is made a certain strength of patience, of which is said in another Psalm, “But my soul shall be subjected to God, for of Him is my patience.”…Wherein dost thou glory?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In tribulations, knowing that tribulation worketh patience.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See how that strength is fashioned within in his heart: “because the love of God is shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Ghost which is given unto us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So is fashioned and made strong that hidden bone, that it maketh us even to glory in tribulations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But to men we seem wretched, because that which we have within is hidden from them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And my substance is in the lower parts of the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, in flesh is my substance, yet have I a bone within, which Thou hast fashioned, such as to cause me never to yield to any persecutions of this lower region, where still my substance is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what great matter is it, if an Angel be brave?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is a great matter, if flesh is brave.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whence is flesh brave, whence is an earthen vessel brave, save because in it is made a bone in secret?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 16. …“Thine eyes did see Mine imperfect one, and in Thy book shall all be written” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 16), not only the perfect, but also the imperfect.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not the imperfect fear, only let them advance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor yet, because I have said, “let them not fear,” let them love their imperfection, and remain there, where they are found.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them advance, as far as in them lieth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Daily let them add, daily let them approach; yet let them not fall back from the Body of the Lord: that, compacted in one Body and among these members, they may be counted worthy to have that said of them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “By day shall they wander, and none among them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Day” was yet on earth, even our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence He said, “Walk while ye have the day.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “by day shall” His imperfect ones “wander.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They too thought that our Lord Jesus Christ was only man, that He had not within Him the hidden Godhead, that He was not secretly God, but that He was that only which was seen: this they too thought.…But what is, “In the day they shall wander”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall they perish?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where then is, “In Thy book shall all be written”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When then did they “wander in the day”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When they understood not the Lord set upon earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But to me Thy friends are made very honourable, O God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 17); those very ones, who “wandered in the day, and none was in them,” became Thy friends, and were made very honourable to me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That bone was made in them in secret after the resurrection of the Lord, and they suffered for His Name, at whose death they had been amazed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mightily strengthened were their chieftainships.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They became Apostles, they became leaders of the Church, they became rams leading their flocks, “mightily strengthened.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will number them, and they shall be multiplied above the sand” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By means of them, who “wandered in the day,” lo! there has been born all this great multitude, which now is like the sand innumerable, save by God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He said, “they shall be multiplied above the sand,” and yet He had said, “I will number them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The very same who are numbered, “shall be multiplied above the sand.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For by Him is the sand numbered, by whom “the very hairs of our head are numbered.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have risen, and yet am I with Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Already have I suffered, saith He, already have I been buried; lo!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have risen, and not yet do they understand that I am with them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Yet am I with Thee,” that is, not yet with them, for not yet do they recognise Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thus do we read in the Gospel, that after the resurrection of oar Lord Jesus Christ, when He appeared to them, they did not at once know Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is another meaning also: “I have risen, and yet am I with Thee,” as though He would signify this present time, wherein He is as yet hidden at the right hand of the Father, before He is revealed in the brightness, wherein He shall come to judge the quick and the dead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And then He telleth what meanwhile, during this whole time when He already has risen, and remaineth still with the Father, He suffereth by the intermixture of sinners in His Body, the Church, and by the separation of heretics.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If Thou, O God, shalt slay the sinners (since Thou shalt say in Thy thought, Depart from Me, ye men of blood), they shall receive in vanity their cities” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The words seem to be connected in this order; “If Thou, O God, shall slay the sinners, they shall receive in vanity their cities.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus are sinners slain, because, “having their understandings darkened, they are alienated from the life of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For on account of elation they lose confession, and so they are slain, and in them is fulfilled what Scripture saith, “Confession perisheth from the dead, as from one that is not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And so “they receive in vanity their cities,” that is, their vain peoples, who follow their vanity; when, puffed up by the name of righteousness, they persuade men to burst the bond of unity, and blindly and ignorantly follow them, as being more righteous.…But now the Body of Christ, the Church, saith, Why do the proud speak falsely against me, as though I were stained by other men’s sins, and so, by separating themselves, “receive in vanity their cities”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Have not I hated those who hated Thee, Lord?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do those who are worse themselves require of me to separate myself in body as well as spirit from the wicked, so as to root up the wheat, together with the tares, before the time of harvest, that before the time of winnowing I lose my power of enduring the chaff; that before all the different sorts of fishes are brought to the end of the world, as to the shore, to be separated, I tear the nets of peace and unity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are the sacraments which I receive, those of evil men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do I; by consent, communicate in their life and deeds?…But where is, “Love your enemies”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it because He said “yours,” not “God’s”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Do good to them that hate you.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith not, “who hate God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So he followeth the pattern, and saith, “Have not I hated those who hated Thee; Lord?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith not, “Who have hated me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And at Thine enemies did I waste away.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thine,” he said, not “mine.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But those who hate us and are enemies unto us, only because we serve Him, what else do they but hate Him, and are His enemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ought we then to love such enemies as these?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or do not they suffer persecution for God’s sake, to whom it is said, “Pray for them that persecute you”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe then what followeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With a perfect hatred did I hate them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “with a perfect hatred”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I hated in them their iniquities, I loved Thy creation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This it is to hate with a perfect hatred, that neither on account of the vices thou hate the men, nor on account of the men love the vices.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For see what he addeth, “They became mine enemies.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not only as God’s enemies, but as his own too doth he now describe them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then will he fulfil in them both his own saying, “Have not I hated those that hated Thee, Lord,” and the Lord’s command, “Love your enemies”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How will he fulfil this, save with that “perfect hatred,” that he hate in them that they are wicked, and love that they are men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in the time even of the Old Testament, when the carnal people was restrained by visible punishments, how did Moses, the servant of God, who by understanding belonged to the New Testament, how did he hate sinners when he prayed for them, or how did he not hate them when he slew them, save that he “hated them with a perfect hatred”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For with such perfection did he hate the iniquity which he punished, as to love the manhood for which he prayed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since then the Body of Christ is in the end to be severed in body also from the unholy and wicked, but now meanwhile groaneth among them, what doeth the “love of Christ among the daughters, as the lily among thorns”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are her words? what her conscience? what is the “appearance of the king’s daughter within”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, hear what she saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Prove me, O God, and know my heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do Thou, O God, Thou prove me, Thou know; not man, not an heretic, who neither knoweth how to prove, nor can know my heart, whereas Thou provest, and knowest that I consent not to the deeds of the wicked, while they think that I can be defiled by the sins of others; so that, while I in my long wandering do what I mourn in another Psalm, that is, while I “labour for peace among them that hate peace,” until I come to that Vision of peace, which is called Jerusalem, “which is the mother of us all,” the city “eternal in the heavens;” they, contending, and falsely accusing and separating themselves, may “receive,” not, evidently, in eternity, but “in vanity, their cities.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe what followeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And see,” saith he, “if there be any way of wickedness in me, and lead me in the way everlasting” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Search,” he saith, “my paths,” that is, my counsels and thoughts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What else saith he, but “lead me in Christ”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who is “the way everlasting,” save He that is the life everlasting?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For everlasting is He who said, “I am the Way, and the Truth, and the Life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then thou findest anything in my way which displeaseth Thine eyes, since my way is mortal, do Thou “lead me in the way everlasting,” wherein is no iniquity; for even “if any man sin, we have an Advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous; and He is the propitiation for our sins;” He is “the Way everlasting” without sin; He is the Life everlasting without punishment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are great mysteries, brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How doth the Spirit of God speak with us? how doth it make us delights in this night?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this, we ask you, brethren, whence are they sweeter, the darker they are?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He mixeth us our potion after His love, in certain wondrous ways.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He maketh His own sayings wondrous, so that while we were speaking what ye already knew, yet forasmuch as it was dug out of passages which seemed obscure, the knowledge itself seemed to be made new.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did ye not know, brethren, that the wicked are to be tolerated in the Church, and schisms not to be made?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did ye not already know, that within those nets which hold both good and bad fishes, we must abide even to the shore, nor must the nets be burst, because on the shore the good shall be separated into vessels, and the bad thrown away?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye know this already; but these verses of this Psalm ye did not understand; that which ye did not understand is explained; that which ye knew has been renewed.
